Class TransactionEventListener

java.lang.Object
com.bank.core.infrastructure.in.amqp.TransactionEventListener
@Component public class TransactionEventListener extends Object
Inbound AMQP adapter that consumes messages from the transaction event queues.

This component listens on two queues bound to the banking.events exchange:

  • notification.queue — simulates dispatching a customer notification (email, SMS, push notification) after a successful transfer.
  • fraud-check.queue — simulates submitting the transaction to a fraud-detection pipeline for asynchronous risk assessment.

Both listeners are intentionally lightweight stubs. In a production system they would delegate to dedicated notification and fraud-detection services. Dead-letter queues (DLQ) are configured in RabbitMQConfig to capture messages that exhaust their retry budget.

Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

  • Constructor Details

    • TransactionEventListener

      public TransactionEventListener(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry meterRegistry)
      Constructs a TransactionEventListener with the required metrics registry.
      Parameters:
      meterRegistry - the Micrometer registry for consumer metrics

  • Method Details

    • onNotification

      @RabbitListener(queues="${banking.rabbitmq.queue.notification}", containerFactory="retryableContainerFactory") public void onNotification(String payload)
      Processes a transaction-completed event for the purpose of customer notification.

      Consumes from notification.queue. The containerFactory named "retryableContainerFactory" applies a back-off retry policy before forwarding a failed message to the Dead Letter Queue.

      Parameters:
      payload - the JSON payload of the completed transaction event

    • onFraudCheck

      @RabbitListener(queues="${banking.rabbitmq.queue.fraud-check}", containerFactory="retryableContainerFactory") public void onFraudCheck(String payload)
      Processes a transaction-completed event for the purpose of fraud analysis.

      Consumes from fraud-check.queue. The containerFactory named "retryableContainerFactory" applies a back-off retry policy before forwarding a failed message to the Dead Letter Queue.

      Parameters:
      payload - the JSON payload of the completed transaction event
